problem_statement: Flight search applies the max_price filter in the wrong direction: GET /flights/search?max_price=200 is returning flights with prices above 200 instead of filtering to flights priced at or below 200.
goal: Fix flight search so max_price acts as an upper bound and only returns flights whose price is less than or equal to the requested maximum.
acceptance_criteria: Calling GET /flights/search?max_price=200 returns only flights with price <= 200.
Flights with price > 200 are excluded from results.
Existing search behavior for other filters remains unchanged.
constraints: Smallest likely fix is in backend/app/services/flight_service.py, specifically search_flights.
Do not change API contract unless necessary.
Keep logic consistent with any existing min/max price filtering semantics.
unknowns: Whether the bug is caused by a comparison operator inversion in search_flights or by request parameter parsing before the service layer.
Whether tests already cover max_price and need updating or a new regression test is required.
repo_summary: Backend service layer appears to contain flight search logic; relevant candidates also include the flights route, flight schema/model, and flight service tests.

targeted_symbols.reason: Issue description and affected file point directly to this search implementation; likely contains the inverted max_price comparison.
Useful to confirm query parameter wiring and whether max_price is passed correctly into the service layer.
Best place to verify existing coverage and add a regression test for max_price filtering.

localization_rationale: The issue explicitly names backend/app/services/flight_service.py (search_flights) as the affected file.
The observed behavior strongly suggests an inverted comparison on the max_price filter rather than a broader schema or route problem.
The route handler and tests are the minimal adjacent areas needed to validate parameter flow and prevent regression.
